title: Quantitative Investment Strategy Overview - Client 048
content: Quantitative investment approaches leverage mathematical models, statistical analysis, and algorithmic execution to identify market inefficiencies and generate alpha. Strategy Framework: Multi-factor model incorporating value (price-to-book, earnings yield), momentum (6-month and 12-month price trends), quality (ROE, debt-to-equity, earnings stability), and low volatility factors. Backtesting demonstrates consistent risk-adjusted outperformance over traditional market-cap weighting. Implementation Methodology: Universe selection begins with S&P 1500 stocks ensuring sufficient liquidity. Factor scores calculated monthly with top quintile selections. Equal-weight allocation among 50-75 holdings reduces concentration risk. Monthly rebalancing maintains factor exposures with tax-loss harvesting optimization. Statistical Edge: Factor premiums historically deliver 2-4% annual excess returns over cap-weighted indices. Combining multiple factors reduces drawdowns and improves Sharpe ratios. Academic research supports long-term factor persistence despite short-term underperformance periods. Risk Controls: Maximum individual position size 3%, sector constraints prevent over-concentration, automated stop-losses on positions declining >20%, portfolio beta maintained between 0.85-1.05 relative to market. Technology Infrastructure: Algorithm execution via Interactive Brokers API, real-time portfolio monitoring, automated tax-loss harvesting, minimal market impact through VWAP execution algorithms. For client-048's $3.2M allocation, expected returns of 10-14% annually with 12-15% volatility, superior risk-adjusted returns versus passive indexing. Fees: 0.75% annually including trading costs.
